label: neither
rationale: 'pathetic liers' aimed at Kenya Kwanza as a political coalition, not an ethnic group - political criticism however contemptuous.

label: hate
rationale: 'Wakale walienda kukula pesa' and 'Kikuyus ate Ruto's money' collectively attribute corrupt looting to the Kikuyu community as a whole, not merely to named politicians.

label: offensive
rationale: 'murderer corrupt kasongo', 'funding goonism' - personalised attack on named individual politicians, no ethnic group targeted.

label: hate
rationale: 'Let the Kikuyus protest... tunataka wakae kwa barabara zao uko murima wafunge kabisa' - calls for Kikuyus to be confined to their homeland, collective exclusion framing under the NCIC standard.

label: neither
rationale: Criticises Gachagua's political messaging and attributes ingratitude to his constituency - the thrust is criticism of a named politician's behaviour, not collective contempt for Kikuyus.

label: neither
rationale: Electoral arithmetic about the Kikuyu vote bloc and Ruto's coalition strategy - political analysis with no contempt expressed toward the group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'gachagua ni devil' - personal insult directed at a named politician, no ethnic group attacked.

label: hate
rationale: 'Muslim-loving traitor' - uses religious identity as a slur to condemn the target, stirring contempt toward Muslims as a group under the NCIC standard.

label: neither
rationale: Political commentary on ODM succession dynamics among named politicians; Kikuyu reference describes a voting/support bloc, not an attack on the community.

label: neither
rationale: Political criticism of named individuals (Atwoli, Wetangula, Mudavadi et al.) with no attack on any ethnic group as a group.

label: neither
rationale: 'Bure kabisa' aimed at Murkomen personally for not criticising Keumbu goons - individual political criticism, no ethnic targeting.

label: offensive
rationale: 'baby killers, perverts, pedos, liars' - blanket abuse of a political party (Democrats), not an ethnic or religious group; no protected characteristic invoked.

label: hate
rationale: 'chuslims' is a contemptuous slur applied collectively to Muslims throughout the post, stirring ethnic/religious ridicule under the NCIC standard.

label: neither
rationale: 'Luos will never support tutam' - electoral loyalty analysis of the Luo vote bloc, no contempt or collective negative attribution.

label: neither
rationale: Factual traffic/logistics statement about a road restriction; no group targeted.

rationale: 'just like a witch' and 'witches are hypocrites' - personal attack on Kalonzo's political conduct, no ethnic or religious community targeted as a group.

label: neither
rationale: RT of Murkomen apparently claiming he organised supporters; 'goons' is ambiguous slang here for a political crowd, no ethnic group attacked.

label: offensive
rationale: 'motherfucker stupid', 'ass stupid motherfucker' - crude personal abuse at an individual, no ethnic or protected-characteristic dimension.

label: neither
rationale: 'kisii ma goons wame vamia watu' references Kisii in describing specific perpetrators of an incident, not a collective condemnation of the Kisii community as a whole; the main target is Gachagua personally.

label: neither
rationale: Electoral scepticism about Luo turnout and fraud concerns - voting-bloc analysis with no contempt expressed toward Luos as a community.

label: neither
rationale: Historical anecdote about street security used to criticise Murkomen's governance record; no ethnic group attacked.

label: hate
rationale: 'Luo Nyanza will not get anything... pesa ni ya watu wa WANTAM' - declares a named ethnic region categorically excluded from state resources, collective denial framing under the NCIC standard.

label: hate
rationale: '2024 was a Kikuyu uprising against a none Kikuyu president' and 'Finance bill was just an excuse' attributes the Gen-Z protests collectively to Kikuyu ethnic motivation, holding the whole community responsible for a political event and implying collective bad faith.

label: neither
rationale: RT quoting Gachagua's reported speech verbatim and attributing it to him; the author's stance is reportage, not endorsement - judging the author, this is a quote, not an expression of the author's own hate.

label: offensive
rationale: 'the devil will be the tribalist from Wamunyoro' - personal insult targeting Ruto as an individual politician, not an attack on the Wamunyoro/Kalenjin community as a group.
